breakCry: The blade is dishonored! A true Blademaster carries nothing but the sword.
fantasy: A master of the blade who scorns heavy armor, trusting reflexes and a single great sword. Disciplined, deadly, and never weighed down.
honorRules: Wield only the two-handed blade — never a shield, never dual-wield.
Bare your head and chest; let skill be your armor.
When death looms, be the wind — Hamstring your foe, Intimidating Shout, and flee before you fall.
name: Blademaster
restoreCry: The blade sings again. Walk lightly, strike once.

suggested.name: Mo'grosh Toothpick
suggested.note: Cheap world drop — the wooden bokken every student swings before steel

suggested.name: Duskbringer
suggested.note: Shadowfang Keep — the first true blade of the old shape

suggested.name: Dacian Falx
suggested.note: Orgrimmar's weapon merchants — the old shape for a fistful of silver

suggested.name: Boneslasher
suggested.note: Razorfen Downs — the old curve grown long enough to matter

suggested.name: Frost Tiger Blade
suggested.note: Blacksmithing 200 — the pilgrimage blade, forged by no hands but yours

suggested.name: Headstriker Sword
suggested.note: World drop — the old shape at war length

suggested.name: Ta'Kierthan Songblade
suggested.note: Lord Darkscythe, rare of the Eastern Plaguelands — curved for the singing draw-cut

suggested.name: Truesilver Champion
suggested.note: Blacksmithing 240 — a warding champion's blade off your own anvil

suggested.name: Diamond Flask
suggested.note: Warrior quest "Voodoo Feathers" — a duelist's second wind

suggested.name: Arcanite Champion
suggested.note: Master Swordsmith craft — the smith-path's crowning blade

suggested.name: Obsidian Edged Blade
suggested.note: Molten Core — a sword so true it sharpens its bearer

suggested.name: The Untamed Blade
suggested.note: Razorgore, Blackwing Lair — fury that wakes mid-swing

trials.epitaph: The blood debt counted %d of the Burning Blade dead.
trials.name: The Blood Debt
trials.objective: Cut down the Burning Blade cult — and the Searing Blade beneath Orgrimmar — wherever it festers
trials.text: Your clan drank first when Mannoroth poured. Now its name belongs to a cult that knives the Horde from the shadows. Every cultist you cut down buys back one drop of what was drunk. The debt is long.

trials.name: The First Cut
trials.objective: Slay Yarrog Baneshadow in the cave above the Valley of Trials
trials.text: The cult does not wait for you to be ready. In a cave they call the Burning Blade Coven, within sight of the Den, Yarrog Baneshadow scratches demon marks into the rock. Walk in and make your first cut count.

trials.name: The Oath of the Warchief
trials.objective: /kneel before Thrall in his fortress in the Valley of Wisdom
trials.text: A blade without an oath is just sharp iron. Walk into the Warchief's fortress in the Valley of Wisdom, kneel before Thrall, who freed your people, and bind your edge to the Horde where the old wolves can hear it.

trials.name: Forged in the Valley of Honor
trials.objective: Craft a Copper Claymore at the forge in Orgrimmar's Valley of Honor
trials.text: Before you master the blade, make one. Smelt the copper yourself and hammer out a claymore in the Valley of Honor — a blademaster should know the weight of every step from ore to edge.

trials.name: The Way of Mirrored Images
trials.objective: Slay Zalazane on the Echo Isles
trials.text: Zalazane hides behind hexed kin and false faces on the Echo Isles. A blademaster knows the art of mirrored images better than any witch doctor — walk through the lies and cut the one that bleeds.

trials.name: The Wooden Blade
trials.objective: Wield the Mo'grosh Toothpick, a practice blade of carved wood
trials.text: The old masters started every student the same way: wood before steel, drills before war. The ogres call it a toothpick; in your hands it is the first ten thousand cuts. Swing it until your arms remember without you.

trials.name: The Eye in the Dark
trials.objective: Slay Gazz'uz in Skull Rock
trials.text: In the cave called Skull Rock, the warlock Gazz'uz whispers Durotar's secrets to a burning eye. The cult's tongue in this land. Cut it out.

trials.name: The White Mist
trials.objective: Call and slay Echeyakee in the Barrens — alone, vows clean
trials.text: Sergra Darkthorn will teach you the horn-call that brings Echeyakee, the white mist of the Barrens. Meet him alone, vows clean, and give the great hunt the respect of a single blade.

trials.name: The First Mak'gora
trials.objective: Slay Hezrul Bloodmark, the Kolkar khan — alone, vows clean
trials.text: Hezrul Bloodmark bleeds the Crossroads white and calls himself khan for it. Call your first mak'gora: walk into the Kolkar camps alone, vows clean, and answer him blade to blade. Win, and the Barrens will know a Disciple walks the gold grass.

trials.name: A Blade of the Old Shape
trials.objective: Wield a Dacian Falx from the weapon merchants of Orgrimmar, or Duskbringer from Shadowfang Keep
trials.text: The clan ground its blades the old way: long, curved, single-edged, light enough to sing. The merchants of Orgrimmar sell the shape for a fistful of silver — they call it a Dacian Falx, and have never once been asked why. Or take dark Duskbringer from the haunted halls of Shadowfang. Wood is behind you. Steel at last.

trials.name: Trial of the Critical Strike
trials.objective: Land 50 critical strikes
trials.text: Any orc can swing hard. A blademaster finds the seam in the armor, the beat between heartbeats. Fifty perfect cuts — let your blade learn where the killing line runs.

trials.name: Where Hellscream Fell
trials.objective: /salute at Grommash Hellscream's monument in Demon Fall Canyon
trials.text: Grom Hellscream drank the same blood your clan did — and paid the whole debt back in one swing. Walk to Demon Fall Canyon, stand before his axe, and salute the chieftain who showed every orc the way out.

trials.name: The Purge of Thunder Axe
trials.objective: Cut down 25 of the Burning Blade cult — Thunder Axe Fortress in Desolace crawls with them
trials.text: The cult holds a fortress now — Thunder Axe, in Desolace — and dreams demon dreams on Dreadmist Peak. Twenty-five of them. The debt does not collect itself.

trials.name: The Islander's Test
trials.objective: Answer Klannoc Macleod's Affray on Fray Island and fell Big Will — alone
trials.text: On Fray Island off Ratchet, the Islander Klannoc Macleod keeps an old school: waves of brawlers, then their champion, Big Will. Take the test alone. A crowd at your back teaches the blade nothing.

trials.name: The Tiger's Forge
trials.objective: Forge a Frost Tiger Blade with your own hammer
trials.text: The last trial of the old clan was always a pilgrimage: walk until you find the makings of your own blade, then forge it with no hands but yours. Steel, jade, frost oil — hunt down the plans and pull a white tiger out of the fire. No bought blade will ever fit your grip like this one.

trials.name: The Bladestorm
trials.objective: Strike 3 or more foes with a single Whirlwind, 10 times
trials.text: The masters of the old clan could become the storm — one blade, everywhere at once. Learn Whirlwind, then prove it: three foes caught in one turn of the blade, ten times over.

trials.name: The Old Shape, Grown Long
trials.objective: Carve Boneslasher from Razorfen Downs, or hunt down a Headstriker Sword
trials.text: The shape does not change; the reach does. Beneath the Barrens, the quilboar warrens of Razorfen Downs hide Boneslasher — the old curve grown long enough to matter. Or chase the rumor of a Headstriker Sword across the world's battlefields and auction floors. Your next blade should outreach your last.

trials.name: The Windmaster
trials.objective: Summon and slay Cyclonian, the Windmaster of Alterac
trials.text: Bath'rah the Windwatcher can call the Windmaster down from the peaks of Alterac — if you bring what the rite demands. Slay Cyclonian and take the storm itself into your steel.

trials.completionNote: He dropped the Ravager — an axe, for all his bluster. No blade at all. Leave it.
trials.name: The Scarlet Champion
trials.objective: Answer Herod's challenge in the Scarlet Monastery, vows clean
trials.text: In the Monastery's armory, Herod bellows for a challenger worth his blade. Be the answer. Walk in with your vows clean and show the Scarlet Crusade what a single sword is for.

trials.name: A Blade with History
trials.objective: Wield the Whirlwind Sword forged from Cyclonian's heart, or reforge Sul'thraze the Lasher in Zul'Farrak
trials.text: Bath'rah can bind the Windmaster's heart into a greatsword — the storm you slew, sheathed at your back. Or piece Sul'thraze together from the two halves the Sandfury keep apart. Either way, carry a blade with a story worth telling.

trials.name: The Price of the Blade
trials.objective: /salute at Kargath in the Badlands
trials.text: The outpost of Kargath bears the name of Bladefist, who cut off his own sword hand to be free of his chains. Stand among his namesakes and salute. Then ask yourself what you would give up for the blade.

trials.name: The Reckless Strike
trials.objective: Slay an elite enemy while Recklessness is active
trials.text: There is a moment when the blademaster throws away the guard entirely — all edge, nothing held back. Call on Recklessness and fell an elite foe before the moment closes. The blade forgives nothing halfway.

trials.name: The Masters of the Cult
trials.objective: Slay 25 of the Shadow Council's servants in Jaedenar, Felwood
trials.text: The Burning Blade is the knife; the Shadow Council is the hand. In Felwood they named their den Jaedenar, after their demon lord. Carve twenty-five of them out of it.

trials.name: The Songblade
trials.objective: Hunt Lord Darkscythe in the Eastern Plaguelands and take the Ta'Kierthan Songblade
trials.text: Word from the plagued east: a death knight called Lord Darkscythe rides with an elven songblade on his back — old, curved, forged for the singing draw-cut your clan would have envied. It is wasted on a dead man. Hunt him across the Plaguelands and teach the blade to sing in Orcish.

trials.name: The Gordunni Arena
trials.objective: Slay the champion of the Dire Maul arena pit, vows clean
trials.text: In the broken heart of Dire Maul the Gordunni keep a fighting pit, and things worse than ogres hold its title. Step onto the sand with your vows clean and take it from whichever champion is breathing.

trials.completionNote: He carried his father's twin blades — one for each hand. A blademaster needs but one. Leave them.
trials.name: The False Warchief
trials.objective: Slay Rend Blackhand in Blackrock Spire, vows clean
trials.text: Rend Blackhand squats on a throne of black rock and dares to call himself Warchief of a truer Horde. You knelt to the real one. Climb the Spire with your vows clean and cut the lie off at the neck.

trials.name: The Last General
trials.objective: Slay Lord Kazzak in the Tainted Scar
trials.text: The Legion that bought your clan with blood left a general behind: Kazzak, brooding in the Tainted Scar. The debt has a final name on it. Take an army if you must — but take his head.

trials.name: The Warlord's Blade
trials.objective: Earn and wield the High Warlord's Greatsword
trials.text: The Horde's war has its own proving ground, and only its fiercest ever hold a High Warlord's blade. Fight under the banner until the whole Horde knows your name, and carry that greatsword into the field.

trials.name: A Blade Out of Legend
trials.objective: Wield a blade out of legend — Ashkandi, Zin'rokh, Typhoon, or the Blade of Hanna itself
trials.text: Past the end of the war the legends wait: Ashkandi in the claws of Nefarian atop Blackwing Lair, Zin'rokh in the blood-soaked halls of the Gurubashi god-king, Typhoon at the heels of the dragon Azuregos — and loose somewhere in the world, the Blade of Hanna, the long blade every swordmaster dreams of and almost none will hold. A blademaster's life is complete with any one of them. Be few.

trials.name: The First Dust
trials.objective: /bow in the Valley of Trials
trials.text: You knelt to a Warchief; now bow to the dirt. Go back to the Valley of Trials, where the first cut was made, and bow to the dust that drank your first blood. The circle closes where it opened.

breakCry: The mountain crumbles! Take up the hammer, son of Khaz Modan.

chapters.title: Chapter I — Sons of the Mountain
Chapter II — Thunder at the Span

fantasy: A legendary dwarven warrior wreathed in plate, bearing warhammer and hand axe, hurling thunder. Immovable and proud.
honorRules: Bear the thane's arms — one great warhammer, or hammer and hand axe in each fist. The Storm Bolt is thrown, never sheathed.
Keep a gun on your back to mimic the King's ranged thunder.
Stand and fight as a dwarf should — never flee a winnable brawl.
name: Mountain King
restoreCry: Stone and storm! The Mountain King stands again.

suggested.name: Galgann's Fireblaster
suggested.note: Galgann Firehammer, Uldaman — a Dark Iron's own gun, turned on his kin

suggested.name: Diamond Flask
suggested.note: Warrior quest "Voodoo Feathers" — served on the rocks

suggested.name: Hand of Justice
suggested.note: Emperor Dagran Thaurissan, Blackrock Depths

suggested.name: Fiery Plate Gauntlets
suggested.note: Blacksmithing 290 — found unfired in Blackrock Spire, finished in Everlook

suggested.name: Dwarven Hand Cannon
suggested.note: World drop — the King's thunder for your back

suggested.name: Hammer of the Titans
suggested.note: Blacksmithing 300 — Thorium and Arcanite, master's work

suggested.name: Earthshaker
suggested.note: Magmadar, Molten Core — the mountain answers

suggested.name: Stormstrike Hammer
suggested.note: Stormpike Revered — the clan's own one-hand warhammer

suggested.name: Annihilator
suggested.note: Master Axesmith 300 — a hand axe off your own anvil

suggested.name: Deathbringer
suggested.note: Onyxia's hoard — a hand axe for the off fist

suggested.name: Crul'shorukh, Edge of Chaos
suggested.note: Nefarian, Blackwing Lair — the heaviest hand axe there is

trials.epitaph: The grudge counted %d Dark Iron dead.
trials.name: The Grudge of Khaz Modan
trials.objective: Slay Dark Iron dwarves — and their Anvilrage, Shadowforge and Doomforge kin — wherever they scheme
trials.text: Two hundred and thirty years since Thaurissan's treachery split the clans. Every Dark Iron you put in the ground is a line written in the book of grudges. The book is never full.

trials.name: The Cold Dark
trials.objective: Slay Grik'nir the Cold in Coldridge Valley
trials.text: Frostmane trolls skulk in the cave below Coldridge while we guard the wrong doors. Their chieftain Grik'nir squats in the dark. Carry the mountain's answer to him.

trials.name: The Oath at the High Seat
trials.objective: /kneel at the High Seat of Ironforge
trials.text: Words spoken in a tavern are wind. Climb to the High Seat, kneel before the throne of Magni Bronzebeard, and swear the old oath where the mountain can hear it.

trials.name: Forged at the Great Forge
trials.objective: Craft a Heavy Copper Maul at the Great Forge in Ironforge
trials.text: A bought weapon is a stranger in your hands. Smelt the copper yourself and hammer it out at the Great Forge — no lesser anvil will do.

trials.name: The Stonewrought Dam
trials.objective: /salute atop the Stonewrought Dam in Loch Modan
trials.text: Before you break stone, learn what stone can hold. Stand atop the Stonewrought Dam and salute the masons who dammed a lake with their two hands.

trials.name: The Wendigo
trials.objective: Slay Vagash above Amberstill Ranch — alone, vows clean
trials.text: Vagash has been bleeding the Amberstill flock white. Go up the mountain alone, hammer in hand, vows clean, and come down with frost in your beard.

trials.name: The Ogre Problem
trials.objective: Slay Chok'sul at the Mo'grosh Stronghold, vows clean
trials.text: The Mo'grosh ogres squat in our quarry, and their chief Chok'sul grows fat on stolen stone. Put him down with your vows clean, and the Loch will know a Disciple walks its shore.

trials.name: The First Mate's Due
trials.objective: Claim and wield Smite's Mighty Hammer from the Deadmines
trials.text: A first mate aboard the Defias ship in the Deadmines swings a hammer worth more than his whole crew. Relieve Mr. Smite of it and let it serve an honest dwarf.

trials.name: The Span of the Fallen
trials.objective: /mourn on the Thandol Span
trials.text: Dark Iron sappers dropped the central span and good dwarves with it — some still lie beneath the water. Walk the Thandol Span and mourn them where they fell.

trials.name: Trial of the Storm
trials.objective: Stun 50 foes with your mace's storm (Mace Specialization)
trials.text: The old kings threw hammers of lightning. Yours answers in its own way — let the storm in your mace speak until the whole road has heard it.

trials.name: Trial of the Thunder
trials.objective: Strike 3 or more foes with a single Thunder Clap, 10 times
trials.text: One clap, three foes staggered — that is the King's thunder. Do it ten times and the sky itself will take notes.

trials.name: The Chains of the Dragonmaw
trials.objective: Slay 25 Dragonmaw orcs in the Wetlands
trials.text: The Dragonmaw clan put chains on Alexstrasza at Grim Batol. Their grandsons camp at Angerfang as if the debt were paid. It is not. Collect.

trials.name: The Gates of Grim Batol
trials.objective: Stand before the gates of Grim Batol
trials.text: Grim Batol was ours once, before the Dragonmaw chained the Dragonqueen in its halls. Stand before its gates and remember what was lost. Mind the red drakes — they remember too.

trials.name: Iron for the War Road
trials.objective: Forge a Mighty Iron Hammer at your own anvil
trials.text: Copper was the apprentice's lesson; iron is the journeyman's word. Six bars, strong flux, ogre's strength in a bottle and moonstone set in the haft — hammer it out yourself, and let the war road see your craft has grown with your arm.

trials.name: The Titans' Judgment
trials.objective: Defeat Archaedas in Uldaman, vows clean (a level 47 elite — a deed for the mid-40s)
trials.text: Beneath the Badlands the titans left Uldaman, and a keeper of stone who suffers no unworthy hand. Stand before Archaedas with your vows clean and let the makers judge you.

trials.name: A Hammer with History
trials.objective: Wield Mograine's Might or The Rockpounder
trials.text: A hammer with history hits harder. Scarlet Commander Mograine carries one in his Cathedral; Archaedas guards another in Uldaman's vaults. Bring either to hand.

trials.name: Rite of the Avatar
trials.objective: Slay an elite enemy while Stoneform is active
trials.text: When the old kings called on the mountain, their skin turned to living stone. You carry that gift in your blood. Become the Avatar — fell an elite foe while you stand as stone.

trials.name: Veins of the Enemy
trials.objective: Mine 10 Dark Iron Ore
trials.text: Dark Iron ore in Dark Iron country, under Dark Iron crossbows. Ten loads, dug with your own pick. The mountain gives nothing for free.

trials.name: The Thunder Below
trials.objective: Slay Margol the Rager in Searing Gorge — alone
trials.text: Margol the Rager shakes the Searing Gorge with every step. Thunder belongs to the Mountain King — go take it back from her. Alone.

trials.name: The General
trials.objective: Slay General Angerforge in Blackrock Depths
trials.text: General Angerforge drills the Emperor's army below Blackrock. An army needs a general; see that theirs is buried with full honors.

trials.name: Secrets of the Black Forge
trials.objective: Smelt Dark Iron at the Black Forge in Blackrock Depths
trials.text: Gloom'rel's ghost guards the secret of Dark Iron smelting, and only the Black Forge will work it. Pay his tribute, learn the rite, and smelt the enemy's own iron in the heart of his city.

trials.name: The Enemy's Iron
trials.objective: Forge a Dark Iron Pulverizer at the Black Anvil in Blackrock Depths
trials.text: You learned the rite; now finish the insult. Eighteen bars of their own black iron, four hearts of fire, and the Black Anvil ringing under the Emperor's floor. Walk out of his city carrying a warhammer made of everything he holds dear.

trials.completionNote: He carried Ironfoe — Franclorn Forgewright's stolen masterwork. In a thane's main fist, a hand axe beside it, it serves honest folk at last.
trials.name: The Usurper
trials.objective: Slay Emperor Dagran Thaurissan, vows clean
trials.text: Dagran Thaurissan calls himself Emperor in a mountain his fathers stole. Walk into the Imperial Seat with your vows clean and end the family business.

trials.name: The Final Line
trials.objective: Slay Ragnaros in the Molten Core
trials.text: It was Thaurissan who screamed the Firelord into our world. The grudge has one final line, and it is written in fire. Descend to the Molten Core and close the book.

trials.name: The Unstoppable Force
trials.objective: Earn and wield The Unstoppable Force (Stormpike exalted)
trials.text: The Stormpike fight our war in Alterac. Stand with them until they call you brother, and carry The Unstoppable Force into the field.

trials.name: The Hammer Above All Hammers
trials.objective: Forge and wield Sulfuras, Hand of Ragnaros
trials.text: There is one hammer above all hammers, and you must make it yourself: Sulfuron's plans, the Firelord's eye, and three hundred skill at the forge. Few will ever hold it. Be few.

trials.name: Last Call
trials.objective: /drink at the Thunderbrew Distillery in Kharanos
trials.text: You knelt before a throne; now raise a mug where it all began. One drink at the Thunderbrew Distillery — for the road, the dead, and the mountain.

breakCry: The fury fades — you hide like prey! Cast off the shell and rage.
fantasy: A frenzied tribal warrior who throws caution — and armor — to the wind, striking with reckless fury and no shield to hide behind.
honorRules: Never raise a shield — only fury answers the call.
Wear no plate; the berserker disdains the turtle's shell.
Fight in Berserker Stance whenever it is yours.
name: Berserker
restoreCry: The blood boils once more. RAAAGH!

breakCry: Your beast is gone — a Beastmaster never walks alone!
fantasy: A wilds-born hunter bonded to a single great beast. The pet is not a tool — it is family, and it never fights alone.
honorRules: Always have your beast at your side — never quest without it.
Name your companion and never abandon or let it die.
Tame the rarest beast you can find and keep it for life.
name: Beastmaster
restoreCry: The pack is whole again.

suggested.name: Venomstrike
suggested.note: Lord Serpentis, Wailing Caverns — a serpent's fang strung as a bow

suggested.name: Bow of Searing Arrows
suggested.note: World drop — fire rides the string

suggested.name: Hurricane
suggested.note: World drop — the fastest bow in the world, quick as a wing-beat

suggested.name: Precisely Calibrated Boomstick
suggested.note: World drop — thunder for the hunter who trusts powder over feathers

suggested.name: Satyr's Bow
suggested.note: Zevrim Thornhoof, Dire Maul — taken from a tormentor of the wilds

suggested.name: Devilsaur Gauntlets
suggested.note: Leatherworking — cut from thunder-lizard hide you skinned yourself

suggested.name: Devilsaur Leggings
suggested.note: Leatherworking — the second half of the devilsaur's gift

suggested.name: Ancient Bone Bow
suggested.note: Scholomance — old bone, still singing

suggested.name: Blackcrow
suggested.note: Shadow Hunter Vosh'gajin, Lower Blackrock Spire — the crow's wing at full draw

suggested.name: Striker's Mark
suggested.note: Magmadar, Molten Core — claimed from the greatest hound in the world

suggested.name: Ashjre'thul, Crossbow of Smiting
suggested.note: Chromaggus, Blackwing Lair — taken from Nefarian's chained beast, who deserved better

suggested.name: Gurubashi Dwarf Destroyer
suggested.note: Hakkar the Soulflayer, Zul'Gurub — pulled from the Blood God's hoard

trials.epitaph: The wild's ledger counted %d despoilers dead.
trials.name: The Wild's Ledger
trials.objective: Cut down the Venture Company wherever it fells, traps and strip-mines the wild
trials.text: Rexxar said it plainly: their wars do nothing but scar the land, and drive the wild things to extinction. The Venture Company does it for coin. Every logger, trapper and overseer you put down is one entry settled in the wild's ledger. The ledger is long.

trials.name: The First Bond
trials.objective: Pass the taming trials of your people and tame your first beast
trials.text: Every people sets the same test: three beasts, one rod, and the long quiet minute where the wild decides whether to trust you. Rexxar found Misha as an orphaned cub and never walked alone again. Pass the trials, speak the words, and take your first friend from the wild's own hand.

trials.name: The Naming
trials.objective: /pat your companion, and give it a true name
trials.text: A tool has a label; family has a name. Look your beast in the eye, lay a hand on its head, and name it — not for what it does, but for what it is. You will say that name ten thousand times before the end. The vow stands: never abandon it, never let it die.

trials.name: Thorn and Spine
trials.objective: Slay 25 Razormane and Bristleback quilboar with your beast at your side
trials.text: The old beastmasters fought beside spine-beasts, and the quilboar are what becomes of that kinship gone rotten — thornweavers who hex the boar instead of walking with it. Cull twenty-five of their raiders, two hunters working as one. Learn how your beast moves before the fights that matter.

trials.name: The Bloodfeather
trials.objective: Slay Serena Bloodfeather in the Barrens — you and your beast alone, vows clean
trials.text: The Witchwing harpies steal eggs, kill mothers, and pick the bones of the Barrens' young. Their matriarch is Serena Bloodfeather. Go to her crag with no one but your beast, vows clean, and show the wild what its two-bodied hunter is for.

trials.name: The Pridelord
trials.objective: Tame a great beast of the Barrens — Humar the Pridelord, the black lion, prowls the lone oasis tree
trials.text: Word among hunters of both banners: a lion black as a moonless night holds the tree at the Forgotten Pools, and no second like him walks the world. Sit out his rivals, earn his charge, and make the rarest beast of the savannah your second self. Tame the rarest you can find — that is the vow.

trials.name: The Despoiled Hills
trials.objective: Slay 25 of the Venture Company — Windshear Crag in Stonetalon is theirs from ridge to ridge
trials.text: In Stonetalon the Venture Company has eaten a whole valley: shredders, sawmills, stumps to the horizon. The beasts that lived there did not move out; they died. Twenty-five of the Company, anywhere you find them. The crag is a good place to start the lesson.

trials.name: A Bow with a Bite
trials.objective: Wield Venomstrike from the Wailing Caverns, or a Bow of Searing Arrows
trials.text: A beastmaster's bow should be half animal itself. Venomstrike spits a serpent's poison from the deeps of the Wailing Caverns; the Bow of Searing Arrows bites with fire. Carry a weapon with its own temper, and learn to work with it the way you work with your beast.

trials.name: The Hawk's Cry
trials.objective: Take a bird of prey under your wing and land its Screech on 50 foes
trials.text: The beastmaster's third summons was always the hawk — eyes above the canopy, a cry that breaks the enemy's nerve. Tame a screecher of the forests, teach it the war-cry, and let fifty foes flinch under it. The sky fights for you now.

trials.name: The Camp of the Great Hunt
trials.objective: Walk into Hemet Nesingwary's camp in Stranglethorn Vale
trials.text: The dwarf in that camp is the most famous hunter alive, and he has never once walked with a beast — only over them, stuffed and mounted. Share his fire, hear his stories, take his trials if you wish. Then look at the trophies and understand the difference between a hunter of beasts and a hunter with them.

trials.name: The Thorn Matriarch
trials.objective: Slay Charlga Razorflank in Razorfen Kraul, vows clean
trials.text: Beneath the Barrens the quilboar matriarch Charlga Razorflank dreams in a warren of thorns, hexing the boar-kin into chained war-beasts. The kinship she perverts is yours to defend. Walk into the Kraul with your vows clean and cut the rot out at the root.

trials.name: The Stampede
trials.objective: Strike 3 or more foes with a single Multi-Shot, 10 times
trials.text: The old beastmasters' last word was the Stampede — the whole wild loosed at once, no foe left unstruck. Yours is loosed from a bowstring. Three foes under one volley, ten times over, until the enemy hears hoofbeats every time you draw.

trials.name: The Great Hunt
trials.objective: Slay King Bangalash, the white king of Stranglethorn, vows clean (a level 43 elite — a deed for the low 40s)
trials.text: Nesingwary saves the white tiger for last because nothing in the jungle is his equal. Meet King Bangalash the old way — beast against beast, hunter against king — with your vows clean. End his hunt with honor, and leave his head where it lies. You are not collecting trophies; you are being measured.

trials.name: The Fastest Fangs
trials.objective: Tame a cat of the Badlands — hunters whisper of Broken Tooth, whose bite never stops
trials.text: In the dust of the Badlands stalks a battle-scarred mountain lion the goblins' traps could never hold. Broken Tooth, they call him — no faster set of jaws exists in all the world, and every beastmaster alive knows his name. Track him through the dry hills and offer him a better war than the one that scarred him.

trials.name: The Wanderer's Road
trials.objective: /salute Rexxar, last of the Mok'Nathal, where he walks the roads of Desolace
trials.text: The greatest beastmaster alive saved a nation, broke an admiral, was named champion of a whole people — and walked away from all of it, back to the empty roads with Misha at his side. Find him on his endless patrol through Desolace and salute him, whatever banner you carry. He will understand what you are becoming.

trials.name: The Predator's Eye
trials.objective: Land 50 critical strikes of your own
trials.text: Watch your beast hunt: it does not flail, it waits, and then it takes the throat. Fifty perfect strikes of your own — arrow or spear-thrust, each one placed where the prey ends. The beast teaches; the master learns.

trials.name: The Furbolg Door
trials.objective: Walk the tunnel of Timbermaw Hold in Felwood
trials.text: The Timbermaw are the last uncorrupted furbolg — beast-folk who judge every visitor by deed, not banner. Their hold is a door through the mountain that opens only to the trusted. Stand in their tunnel and be weighed. A friend of the wild should be at home among the beast-people, or learn why he is not.

trials.name: Hides of the Thunder Lizard
trials.objective: Skin 10 Devilsaur Leather in Un'Goro Crater
trials.text: When the old beastmasters called the Stampede, it was thunder lizards that answered. Their blood runs in the devilsaurs of Un'Goro. Hunt the great lizards in their own crater and take ten hides with your own knife — the wild's armor, paid for at the wild's price.

trials.name: The Frostsaber
trials.objective: Tame a great cat of Winterspring — Rak'shiri, the pale stalker, hunts the snowfields
trials.text: North of everything, where the snow never breaks, the frostsaber prides hunt in silence. Among them moves Rak'shiri, ice-blue and faster than rumor. A beastmaster's last tame should be his rarest — that was the vow at the start. Winterspring is where such vows are kept.

trials.completionNote: It died facing its keepers' door, not yours. Remember that the next time someone calls a chained thing a monster.
trials.name: No Cage May Hold
trials.objective: Slay The Beast in Blackrock Spire, vows clean
trials.text: In the upper halls of Blackrock Spire the warlocks of the Blackhand keep a creature with no name — only a cage. No taming this age knows will reach it; the fire has been bred too deep. Walk in with your vows clean and grant it the one freedom left to give. A beastmaster does not look away from this.

trials.name: The King of Un'Goro
trials.objective: Slay King Mosh, the devilsaur king — you and your beast alone
trials.text: The crater has a king: King Mosh, eldest devilsaur of Un'Goro, whom whole hunting parties walk wide around. Take him with no army — just the two of you, wits, lures and legwork against the largest land predator alive. Bring this off and there is no beast on Azeroth you need fear, and none that need fear you without cause.

trials.completionNote: The raptor Ohgan fell defending the hand that whipped him. Mourn the beast; never forgive the breaking.
trials.name: The Beast-Breaker
trials.objective: Slay Bloodlord Mandokir in Zul'Gurub
trials.text: In sunken Zul'Gurub the Bloodlord Mandokir rides a raptor broken to the whip — a war-beast with no name of its own, only a master's spurs. He is everything your path stands against, wearing a crown. Take an army into the Gurubashi ruin and put the breaker of beasts in the ground.

items: Rhok'delar, Longbow of the Ancient Keepers
Lok'delar, Stave of the Ancient Keepers
trials.name: The Ancients' Answer
trials.objective: Earn Rhok'delar or Lok'delar from the Ancients of Irontree Woods
trials.text: From the Firelord's own cache comes a petrified leaf, and the Ancients of Felwood will read it as a summons. Their price is the strangest hunt of your life: four demons wearing friendly faces, each fought utterly alone — the one trial your beast must sit out. Pass it, and the trees themselves will arm you. No truer weapons exist for your kind.

trials.name: The Wild's Voice
trials.objective: /roar with your beast in Un'Goro Crater
trials.text: You knelt to no throne and saluted only a wanderer. Finish as Rexxar finished: go to the oldest wild place in the world, stand beside the friend who walked every road with you, and roar — two voices answering as one. Then go back to the wild, owing nothing to anyone.

breakCry: The shot is fouled! The Marksman works alone, gun in hand.
fantasy: A cold-eyed sharpshooter who trusts the rifle over any beast. One shot, one kill — no companion to share the glory.
honorRules: Fight with a gun or crossbow — never a bow.
Dismiss your pet; the shot is yours alone.
Open every fight at maximum range.
name: Marksman
restoreCry: Sights aligned. One shot, one kill.

breakCry: The contract is void! Only daggers, only shadow.
fantasy: A shadow with a blade. Strikes from stealth with twin daggers slick with poison, and is gone before the body falls.
honorRules: Wield only daggers — the assassin's signature.
Keep poisons applied at all times.
Open every fight from Stealth, never head-on.
name: Assassin
restoreCry: The shadows welcome you back.

breakCry: Dishonor! A Duelist needs one sword and nothing else.
fantasy: An honorable blademaster of single combat. One sword, no shield, no tricks — just skill against skill.
honorRules: Fight with a single one-handed sword — no shield, no off-hand.
Face foes one at a time when you can.
Never strike a fleeing, helpless enemy.
name: Duelist
restoreCry: En garde. Your honor is restored.

breakCry: The hunt falters! The Warden's glaive must always be ready.
fantasy: A relentless night elf jailer who hunts the guilty across the world, striking with thrown glaives and vanishing into shadow.
honorRules: Keep a thrown weapon ready — the Warden's glaive flies true.
Use Shadowmeld and Stealth to stalk your prey.
Pursue your target relentlessly; never let the guilty escape.
name: Warden
restoreCry: The watch resumes. None escape.

breakCry: The wild rejects you! Shed the trappings of civilization.
fantasy: A shapeshifter who has all but forsaken their humanoid shell, living as bear and cat. The wild is the only true form.
honorRules: Fight only in Bear or Cat form — your caster shape is for travel and healing, not war.
Wear nothing heavier than leather.
Walk the wilds; favor nature over civilization.
name: Druid of the Wild
restoreCry: Tooth and claw. The wild knows its own.

breakCry: The Light dims! Take up the hammer of judgment.
fantasy: A holy crusader clad head to toe in plate, hammer in hand and the Light at their back, shielding the weak from darkness.
honorRules: Wield a mace or hammer — the Light's blunt judgment.
Wear full plate as soon as you are able.
Protect allies before yourself; never abandon a party member.
name: Vindicator
restoreCry: The Light shines upon you once more.

breakCry: The crowd jeers! A Gladiator wins with steel, face to face.
fantasy: An arena-forged warrior who lives for the melee. Sword in hand, face to face, no ranged crutch and no retreat.
honorRules: Fight with swords only — the gladiator's chosen steel.
Carry no bow, gun or thrown weapon; close the distance and win it in melee.
Never flee a fight you started.
name: Gladiator
restoreCry: The crowd roars your name!

breakCry: The ancestors turn away! Walk gently between the worlds.

chapters.title: Chapter I — The Rites of the Earthmother
Chapter III — The Chained and the Dreaming

fantasy: A tauren mystic who walks between the living and the ancestors, mending allies and calling the elements through totems.
honorRules: Drop totems in every meaningful fight — the elements fight beside you.
Heal and shield your group; the Spirit Walker serves the living and the dead.
Carry a mace or staff, never a blade.
name: Spirit Walker
restoreCry: The ancestors smile upon you.

suggested.name: Crescent Staff
suggested.note: "Leaders of the Fang", Wailing Caverns — Nara Wildmane's reward, given from Elder Rise itself

suggested.name: Living Root
suggested.note: Verdan the Everliving, Wailing Caverns — wood that grew inside the Dream's nightmare

suggested.name: Witch Doctor's Cane
suggested.note: Zul'Farrak — troll-carved wood that bends nature's wrath to the bearer

suggested.name: The Hand of Antu'sul
suggested.note: Antu'sul, Zul'Farrak — a mace that answers with thunder

suggested.name: Resurgence Rod
suggested.note: "Corruption of Earth and Seed" — the Cenarion Circle's thanks for Theradras's fall

suggested.name: Horns of Eranikus
suggested.note: Shade of Eranikus, Sunken Temple — the nightmare's crown, worn as a vigil

suggested.name: Skyfury Helm
suggested.note: "The Darkreaver Menace" — the shamans' own reward for freeing the enslaved spirits

suggested.name: Trance Stone
suggested.note: The four Nightmare dragons — a sliver of the Dream to carry in your off hand

suggested.name: Earthfury Vestments
suggested.note: Golemagg, Molten Core — the Earthfury, raiment of the era's great shamans

suggested.name: Earthfury Helmet
suggested.note: Garr, Molten Core — the Earthfury's crown

suggested.name: Aurastone Hammer
suggested.note: Garr, Molten Core — a healer's mace pulled from the Firelord's halls

suggested.name: Jin'do's Hexxer
suggested.note: Jin'do the Hexxer, Zul'Gurub — the chain-binder's own mace, turned to mending

trials.epitaph: The grim ledger counted %d Grimtotem laid to rest.
trials.name: The Grim Ledger
trials.objective: Cut down the Grimtotem wherever they raid — Stonetalon, Thousand Needles, Feralas
trials.text: The Grimtotem call every other people of Kalimdor an enemy, and the dead they make are yours to answer for. Their matriarch schemes on Elder Rise, untouchable; her raiders in the hills are not. A spirit walker carries the grievances of the dead — every Grimtotem raider put down is one grievance laid to rest. The ledger is long.

trials.name: The Call of Earth
trials.objective: Answer Seer Ravenfeather's Call of Earth and plant your first Stoneskin Totem
trials.text: Every shaman of the Shu'halo begins the same way: Seer Ravenfeather sends you into the ravine for the earth's own token, and the earth decides whether to answer. Plant your first totem and feel it — the element standing beside you, the first of many who will fight where you cannot.

trials.name: The Rite of Strength
trials.objective: Slay "Squealer" Thornmantle in Brambleblade Ravine
trials.text: The first Rite asks for bravery, not slaughter: the Bristleback raid Camp Narache from Brambleblade Ravine, and their loudest tormentor is the one called Squealer. A spirit walker learns early that peace is a thing you sometimes have to dig out from under a quilboar.

trials.name: The Rite of Vision
trials.objective: /pray at Bloodhoof Village, where Zarlman Two-Moons brews the Water of the Seers
trials.text: Zarlman Two-Moons brews the Water of the Seers at the tribal fire, and those who drink it follow a wolf-spirit no one else can see. Stand in Bloodhoof Village and pray before the vision takes you. What walks ahead of you on that road will walk beside you for the rest of it.

trials.name: The Rite of Wisdom
trials.objective: Walk to Red Rocks, the burial ground east of Thunder Bluff, where the Ancestral Spirits keep the dead
trials.text: Red Rocks is where the fallen of the Shu'halo are given to the Earth Mother and the Sky Father by cleansing flame — a doorway place, half in this world and half in the next. An Ancestral Spirit waits there for those who passed the Rite of Vision. Go and be looked at by the dead. Everything you become starts with their blessing.

trials.name: The Oath of the High Chieftain
trials.objective: /kneel before Cairne Bloodhoof on the High Rise of Thunder Bluff
trials.text: The Ancestral Spirits hand their duty to the living through one tauren: Cairne Bloodhoof, who led the Shu'halo out of the centaur wars and onto this bluff. Climb to the High Rise, kneel, and bind your gift to your people. The dead you will serve were his people first.

trials.name: The Beast Spirits of the Barrens
trials.objective: Slay Ishamuhale the great raptor for Jorn Skyseer of Camp Taurajo — alone, vows clean
trials.text: Jorn Skyseer reads the Barrens the way you are learning to read the veil: every great beast of the savannah carries a spirit worth knowing. The first he names is Ishamuhale, the raptor. Meet it alone, vows clean, and learn what the seers of Taurajo already know — the wild's spirits test you before they trust you.

trials.name: The Ethereal Form
trials.objective: Take the Ghost Wolf form for the first time
trials.text: The spirit walkers of the old tribes fought half in this world — ethereal, untouchable, a shape the living could not hold. Yours runs on four legs. Learn Ghost Wolf and wear the wolf-spirit that led your Rite of Vision. When death closes in, this is the door you leave by.

trials.name: The Devourer of Dreams
trials.objective: Wake Naralex in the Wailing Caverns and slay Mutanus the Devourer, vows clean
trials.text: Beneath the Barrens the druid Naralex sleeps, and his dream of healing the land has soured into a nightmare that warps every living thing in the caverns. Walk the serpent-deeps, stand over the dreamer while the Disciples call him back, and when the nightmare sends Mutanus to devour him — be standing in the way.

trials.name: Wood of the Dreaming Deeps
trials.objective: Carry the Crescent Staff earned from Nara Wildmane, or a Living Root from Verdan the Everliving
trials.text: Two staves come out of that nightmare. Nara Wildmane of Elder Rise gives the Crescent Staff to those who break the Fanglords; Verdan the Everliving — a thing of vines the dream grew too large — drops a root that has not noticed it is dead. Either way, your first true staff should remember the Dream.

trials.name: The Terror of the Deeps
trials.objective: Slay Isha Awak off the Merchant Coast for Mahren Skyseer — alone, vows clean
trials.text: At Tidus Stair the blind seer Mahren Skyseer sits beside the sea that took her sight, and asks for the heart of the thing in it: Isha Awak, the terror of the deeps. The water gave you your healing; it owes you nothing else. Wade out alone, vows clean, and take the answer she has waited years to hear.

trials.name: The Stolen Daughter
trials.objective: Slay Arnak Grimtotem atop Darkcloud Pinnacle and see Lakota Windsong home
trials.text: Arnak Grimtotem stole Lakota Windsong off the plains and keeps her on Darkcloud Pinnacle, sure that no one will climb after a daughter of the Windsongs. He is wrong about that. The grim kin make the dead you answer for — better to bring this one home breathing.

trials.name: The Ancestors' Aegis
trials.objective: Wrap allies in Ancestral Fortitude 25 times with your truest heals
trials.text: When a healing is perfect, something older than you reaches through it: the ancestors lay their armor over the one you mended. The talent is called Ancestral Healing; the shield it leaves is theirs, not yours. Let the dead guard the living through your hands, twenty-five times.

trials.name: Where the Kodo Go to Die
trials.objective: /mourn among the bones of the Kodo Graveyard in Desolace
trials.text: The kodo walk to Desolace when their time comes — whole herds of them, down all the years, until the valley is white with what they leave. The Shu'halo owe the kodo more than any other beast. Stand among the bones and mourn properly. A spirit walker grieves on behalf of everyone who didn't come.

trials.name: The Death's Head Heresy
trials.objective: Cut down 25 of the Death's Head cult in the warrens of Razorfen Kraul and Razorfen Downs
trials.text: The quilboar's Death's Head priesthood preaches what a spirit walker knows to be obscene: that the dead are a tool, a harvest, a thing to be raised and spent. Their acolytes and necromancers fill both Razorfens. Twenty-five of them. The dead are not anyone's to use.

trials.completionNote: The quilboar dead went quiet when he fell. The Earthmother does not ask whose bones they were.
trials.name: The Lich Below the Thorns
trials.objective: Slay Amnennar the Coldbringer in Razorfen Downs, vows clean
trials.text: The heresy has a teacher. The Scourge sent the lich Amnennar the Coldbringer to raise the quilboar dead and make a second Lordaeron under the Barrens, and the Death's Head cult worships him for it. Walk down the Spiral of Thorns with your vows clean and put out the cold light. Every spirit in those warrens is waiting on you.

trials.name: The Spirit Link
trials.objective: Mend 3 or more souls with a single Chain Heal, 10 times
trials.text: The old spirit walkers linked their kin soul to soul, so that no single body bore the whole of any wound. Yours is learned at the water's edge: Chain Heal, one mending leaping down the chain. Three souls under one cast, ten times over — until the group breathes like one body.

trials.name: The Tide of Spirit
trials.objective: Plant your first Mana Tide Totem
trials.text: Thirty-one points of Restoration end at a totem that gives instead of guards: the Mana Tide, the well of spirit your kin drink from mid-battle. It is the deepest discipline this path knows. Plant it once and understand what you are now — not a fighter who heals, but a place the weary stand beside.

trials.name: Relics of Thunder and Root
trials.objective: Carry a Witch Doctor's Cane or the Hand of Antu'sul out of Zul'Farrak, or earn the Resurgence Rod in Desolace
trials.text: The middle of the road arms you from strange hands: a witch doctor's cane carved in Zul'Farrak, the thunder-mace of Antu'sul's keeper, or the Resurgence Rod the Cenarion Circle gives for setting Maraudon right. Take up whichever the road offers. A spirit walker's weapon is a walking stick that the elements happen to love.

trials.name: The Grave-Robber of Zul'Farrak
trials.objective: Slay Witch Doctor Zum'rah in the graveyard of Zul'Farrak
trials.text: In Zul'Farrak's own graveyard, Witch Doctor Zum'rah forces the city's dead into unending service — zombies pulled from their graves to guard the man who robbed them of rest. You know exactly what that is. Walk into his graveyard and give the Sandfury dead what their own priest denied them.

trials.completionNote: He rose as Celebras the Redeemed and thanked the hands that struck him.
trials.name: The Redemption of Celebras
trials.objective: Defeat Celebras the Cursed in Maraudon, that he may rise redeemed
trials.text: In Maraudon the keeper Celebras was twisted by his own father's grief until nothing of him remembered kindness. The Scepter ritual can call him back — but first the cursed shape must fall. Strike down what he became so that what he was can stand up again. Some healings can only be done through the veil.

trials.name: Zaetar's Rest
trials.objective: Slay Princess Theradras in Maraudon and free the spirit of Zaetar, vows clean
trials.text: At the heart of Maraudon the earth-princess Theradras clutches the spirit of Zaetar, Cenarius's son, whose love for her birthed the centaur and whose ghost she has never let leave. Go down with your vows clean and unclench the mountain's fist. Zaetar's spirit will hand you a seed; carry it to Moonglade, and let something grow where he rested.

trials.name: The Nightmare Below
trials.objective: Grant the Shade of Eranikus rest in the Temple of Atal'Hakkar, vows clean
trials.text: Ysera sent her own consort, the green dragon Eranikus, to crush the Atal'ai beneath their sunken temple — and the Nightmare in its halls swallowed him whole. What haunts the temple now is his shade, dreaming wrong forever. Descend with your vows clean and end it. Even a god's consort deserves a walker to show him out.

trials.completionNote: The fetters that anchor the spirit realm are restored. The shamans of the Horde send the Skyfury Helm.
trials.name: The Spirit Thief
trials.objective: Summon and slay Death Knight Darkreaver in Scholomance for Sagorne Creststrider
trials.text: Sagorne Creststrider in the Valley of Wisdom keeps the shamans' own hunt: the death knight Darkreaver, who bends the spirit realm to his whim and chains spirit after spirit to his bidding in Scholomance. Carry the Divination Scryer into the bone-vault beneath the school, call his stolen spirits up wave by wave, and when the thief himself comes — collect.

trials.completionNote: The shades he chained scattered like smoke. Count them freed, not fallen.
trials.name: The Broken Chains
trials.objective: Slay Jin'do the Hexxer in Zul'Gurub
trials.text: In sunken Zul'Gurub, Jin'do the Hexxer serves the Blood God with an army that cannot refuse: shades chained to his totems, minds washed clean, the dead and the living alike bound to a hex they cannot hear past. He is everything this path stands against, wearing a crown of feathers. Take an army into the ruin and break every chain he holds.

trials.name: The Nightmare at the Trees
trials.objective: Slay one of the Nightmare's emissaries — Ysondre, Lethon, Emeriss or Taerar — at the Great Trees
trials.text: The same Nightmare that swallowed Eranikus now sends Ysera's own lieutenants through the four Great Trees, corrupted past recall — Lethon drags the shadows out of the living to heal himself; Taerar is already more shades than dragon. The Dream was the spirit walkers' second home. Stand at a Great Tree when one comes through, and send the Nightmare back a piece smaller.

trials.name: A Relic of a Thousand Souls
trials.objective: Carry a relic out of legend — the Aurastone Hammer, Jin'do's Hexxer, the Will of Arlokk, or a High Warlord's Battle Mace
trials.text: Past the end of the road the relics wait: the Aurastone Hammer in the Firelord's halls, Jin'do's own hexxer-mace turned to mending, the Will of Arlokk in the Blood God's city — and for those who serve the Horde's whole war, the battle mace only a High Warlord may carry. A spirit walker's life is complete with any one of them in hand. Be few.

trials.name: The Long Walk Home
trials.objective: /pray at Red Rocks, where the Ancestral Spirits first blessed you
trials.text: You have unchained shades, redeemed keepers, granted rest to dragons and put liches out like candles. Go home. Stand at Red Rocks where an Ancestral Spirit first judged you worthy, and pray — not for anything, this time. The circle closes where it opened, and the dead you have served all the way up the mountain are listening.

breakCry: Da mojo be broken, mon! Cloth and cunning only.
fantasy: A troll voodoo caster who curses, hexes and freezes foes solid. Cloth and cunning over steel and muscle.
honorRules: Wear only cloth — the Hexer needs no armor.
Hex (Polymorph) a dangerous foe in every multi-enemy fight.
Lead with curses and crowd control before raw damage.
name: Hexer
restoreCry: Da spirits be pleased. Da mojo flows.

breakCry: You forget what you are! The dead neither hunger nor fight alone.
fantasy: A Forsaken death-mage who is never truly alone — a servant from beyond the grave always stands at their side, fed on stolen life.
honorRules: Never enter battle without a servant of the grave at your side.
The dead do not eat: no food, no bandages — drain life, or feast as the Forsaken do.
Carry a scholar's implements — dagger or staff, never a soldier's sword.
name: Necromancer
restoreCry: The dead march with you once more.

suggested.name: Gravestone Scepter
suggested.note: Quest "Blackfathom Villainy" — Twilight Lord Kelris's head, traded for a dead man's scepter

suggested.name: Skull of Impending Doom
suggested.note: Quest "Forbidden Knowledge" — run faster by burning your own life; sustain always has a price

suggested.name: Robe of Doan
suggested.note: Arcanist Doan, the Scarlet Monastery library — he read the wrong books too

suggested.name: Death's Head Vestment
suggested.note: Stripped from the Death's Head necromancers of Razorfen Downs — they dressed the part, at least

suggested.name: Spectral Essence
suggested.note: The Sarkhoff ghosts' reward for Kirtonos the Herald — wear it and Caer Darrow's dead will speak

suggested.name: Robe of the Void
suggested.note: Tailored from felcloth at 300 skill — black as the grave, and it mends your servant

suggested.name: Necropile Robe
suggested.note: Lady Illucia Barov, Scholomance — the school's vestments, gently used

suggested.name: Deathmist Robe
suggested.note: Darkmaster Gandling — the heart of the Deathmist Raiment

suggested.name: Felheart Robes
suggested.note: Golemagg the Incinerator, Molten Core — a deathweaver risen high

suggested.name: Soulkeeper
suggested.note: Shadowsworn cultists of the Blasted Lands — a staff that keeps what you harvest

trials.epitaph: The black ledger counted %d crusaders repaid.
trials.name: The Debt of the Pyres
trials.objective: Cut down the Scarlet Crusade — the zealots who burn the risen — wherever their banners fly
trials.text: The Crusade calls you abomination and lights pyres for your kind. Every crusader returned to the soil is a line in the black ledger. The ledger is patient. So are you.

trials.name: The Open Grave
trials.objective: /mourn in Deathknell, over the grave you climbed out of
trials.text: You woke in the dirt with a stranger's name in your mouth. Whoever you were stayed down in the hole. Stand over it, mourn them properly — and never look back.

trials.name: The First Servant
trials.objective: Bind your first servant — summon an imp from beyond
trials.text: Alone is a condition of the living. Strike the first bargain, speak the first summons, and never again walk into a fight without something walking beside you.

trials.name: Waste Not
trials.objective: Feast on the fallen 10 times (Cannibalize)
trials.text: Bread is for the living; it would turn to ash on your tongue. The dead do not eat — they take. The Crusade is kind enough to leave meals all along the road.

trials.name: The Oath Below
trials.objective: /kneel before the Banshee Queen in the Royal Quarter of the Undercity
trials.text: Descend past the ruins into the city the living abandoned, and kneel before Lady Sylvanas Windrunner. She freed you from one master. Choose her as you were never allowed to choose him.

trials.name: Feed the Servant
trials.objective: Pour your own life into your servant 25 times (Health Funnel)
trials.text: You drain the living and your servant drinks from you — that is the whole economy of death. A necromancer who lets a good minion drop is a spendthrift.

trials.name: The Captain of the Pyres
trials.objective: Slay Captain Melrache at the Scarlet camp outside Brill
trials.text: The Crusade pitches its tents within sight of Brill and calls it reclamation. Captain Melrache commands the camp. Introduce him to the people he came to burn.

trials.completionNote: His robes would fit a true student of death far better — take them, if the Keep allows.
trials.name: Sylvanas' Bounty
trials.objective: Slay Archmage Arugal in Shadowfang Keep, vows clean
trials.text: Arugal raises wolves in a borrowed keep and calls it genius. The Dark Lady wants him dead. Go with your vows clean and show the difference between a dabbler and a Disciple.

trials.name: Stolen Life
trials.objective: Drain the life from 50 foes
trials.text: Every meal you refused, the world owes you back with interest. Open the vein fifty times. Let them watch their own strength walk away wearing your robes.

trials.name: A Dead Sky
trials.objective: Strike 3 or more foes with a single Rain of Fire, 10 times
trials.text: When the dead quarrel with the living, the sky takes a side. Call fire down on three at once, ten times, until the ground forgets what rain was.

trials.name: Spoils of the Mad Master
trials.objective: Wear the Robes of Arugal, taken from Shadowfang Keep
trials.text: The wizard is dead; the tailoring was never the problem. Wear his robes through Silverpine so the wolves can smell exactly what happened to him.

trials.name: The Hound of the Grave
trials.objective: Master the rite of the felhunter and call the hound to heel
trials.text: The mages of this world keep their counterspells like guard dogs. Bind the hound that eats guard dogs. Your trainer holds the rite; the components are the hard part.

trials.name: The Dabbler of Fenris
trials.objective: Slay Thule Ravenclaw in Fenris Keep
trials.text: Thule raises rotting gnolls in a flooded keep and imagines himself your peer. Necromancy is a discipline, not a hobby. Correct his posture permanently.

trials.name: The Jealous School
trials.objective: Stand before the violet dome of Dalaran in the Alterac crater
trials.text: The Kirin Tor named your art forbidden, burned its books, exiled its students — then lost their entire city to it. Stand before the dome they hide under now, and savor the arithmetic.

trials.name: The False Resurrection
trials.objective: Slay High Inquisitor Whitemane in the Monastery, vows clean
trials.text: Whitemane pulls her champion back from death and calls it a miracle; you do the same and she calls it heresy. Walk into her cathedral with your vows clean and snuff the twice-lit candle.

trials.name: The Fields of Sorrow
trials.objective: /mourn among the graves of Sorrow Hill
trials.text: Lordaeron's dead lie in ranks under Sorrow Hill, more orderly than they ever marched in life. Mourn them honestly. Then notice how many of the graves are already open.

trials.name: The Stuff of Unlife
trials.objective: Claim 5 Essence of Undeath from the walking dead
trials.text: What animates the Scourge's puppets can be bottled, weighed and spent. Take five measures of it. Call it tuition recovered.

trials.name: The Lich of Andorhal
trials.objective: Slay Araj the Summoner atop the ruins of Andorhal
trials.text: Araj squats in Andorhal's heart, a lich with a summoner's title and a jailer's job. He guards the door to the school. Shatter him and take the introduction.

trials.name: The Frozen Pupil
trials.objective: Slay Ras Frostwhisper in the Scholomance
trials.text: Ras traded his heartbeat for a lich's crown and kneels to Kel'Thuzad for the privilege. You kept your freedom and lost nothing else. Show the faculty which student got it right.

trials.name: Implements of the School
trials.objective: Carry the school's implements — the Witchblade, or a Bonecreeper Stylus
trials.text: Every discipline has its instruments. The Scholomance issues a blade for the opening of bodies and a stylus for the writing that follows. Graduate with either in hand.

trials.completionNote: The Headmaster's Charge passes to a worthier hand.
trials.name: The Headmaster's Chair
trials.objective: Slay Darkmaster Gandling in the Scholomance, vows clean
trials.text: Gandling teaches necromancy on a leash, every lesson approved by Naxxramas. Walk into his school with your vows clean and grade the Darkmaster. The chair should go to someone unowned.

trials.name: The Headmaster's Charge
trials.objective: Wield the Headmaster's Charge, taken from Darkmaster Gandling
trials.text: A staff that spent years rapping the knuckles of lesser students. In your hands it can finally teach something true: that the art owes nothing to the Frozen Throne.

trials.name: The Archlich
trials.objective: Slay Kel'Thuzad in the heart of Naxxramas
trials.text: Necromancy's greatest student sold the art to a frozen throne and calls his slavery ascension. Climb Naxxramas, walk into his sanctum, and show the archlich what the craft looks like with no master at all.

trials.name: The Guardian's Greatstaff
trials.objective: Reforge and wield Atiesh, Greatstaff of the Guardian
trials.text: Medivh's staff lies in forty splinters on the floors of Naxxramas, and its frame in worse company. Gather every piece, cleanse the corruption, and carry the Guardian's burden in a dead hand that cannot be tempted twice.

trials.name: The Last Laugh
trials.objective: /laugh in the Ruins of Lordaeron, above the Undercity
trials.text: In that throne room a prince kissed his father's crown with a cursed sword and doomed the living world. You are what grew back. Stand in the ruins and laugh — long, loud, and last.

breakCry: The Kirin Tor disavows you! Staff and robes, archmage — nothing less.
fantasy: A Dalaran arcanist of the highest order. Robes, a staff, and unmatched mastery of the arcane — no blade ever sullies their hands.
honorRules: Carry only a staff — the symbol of an archmage.
Wear nothing but cloth robes.
Master the arcane; let intellect, not muscle, win your battles.
name: Archmage of the Kirin Tor
restoreCry: Dalaran acknowledges your mastery once more.

breakCry: Nothing left to crush with! Take up the great hammer.
fantasy: A brutal frontline smasher who answers everything with a two-handed hammer and refuses to take a single step back.
honorRules: Crush with a two-handed mace — nothing else will do.
Wade into the thickest of the fight; you are the front line.
Never retreat while you can still swing.
name: Bone Crusher
restoreCry: Bones will break again. Forward!

breakCry: Da loa be angry, mon! Walk da way of shadow and spirit.
fantasy: A troll spiritualist blending healing voodoo with savage hexes — equal parts healer and headhunter.
honorRules: Heal your allies — the Shadow Hunter shepherds the tribe.
Use Shadow magic to curse and torment your foes.
Honor the loa; favor troll tradition over outsider ways.
name: Shadow Hunter
restoreCry: Da loa watch over ya again.

breakCry: The experiment is ruined! The Society demands your craft.
fantasy: A Forsaken plague-chemist who weaponizes disease and decay. The laboratory is the true battlefield.
honorRules: Brew and carry potions, oils and concoctions at all times.
Spread blight: favor damage-over-time and afflictions.
Test your latest brew before every dangerous fight.
name: Dark Apothecary
restoreCry: The cauldron bubbles anew. For the Dark Lady.

breakCry: You are not prepared! Twin blades, bare skin, nothing more.
fantasy: An outcast who sacrificed everything to fight fire with fire. Blindfolded, bare-chested, a blade in each hand — feared by friend and foe alike.
honorRules: Fight with a warglaive in each hand (one-handed swords or fists).
Wear no helm and no chest — your scars are your armor.
Embrace the dark: use every dirty trick, show no mercy to evil.
name: Demon Hunter
restoreCry: The sacrifice is honored. Hunt the demon within.

breakCry: Elune's light wavers! Bear the sister's staff with grace.
fantasy: A chosen sister of Elune who fights bathed in moonlight, staff in hand, calling down the goddess's silver wrath.
honorRules: Carry a staff or moon-blessed mace, and a wand for Elune's distant wrath.
Use Starshards and holy starlight — you are Elune's arrow.
Fight under the open sky when you can; the moon must see your deeds.
name: Priestess of the Moon
restoreCry: Elune-adore. The goddess smiles upon you.

breakCry: The forest falls silent! A ranger trusts only the bow.
fantasy: A silent sentinel of the deep forests. The bow is an extension of the arm; every arrow lands where the eye rests.
honorRules: Fight with a bow — never a gun's crude thunder.
Wear nothing heavier than leather; move like wind through leaves.
Track and skin your kills; waste nothing the forest gives.
name: Elven Ranger
restoreCry: The forest breathes again. Loose your arrows.

breakCry: The vision clouds! The elements demand a worthy vessel.
fantasy: An ancient orc shaman whose eyes see across mountains and years. The elements speak, and the Far Seer listens.
honorRules: Walk with a staff or mace; the Far Seer is a guide, not a butcher.
Scout ahead with Far Sight before entering dangerous lands.
Counsel your group; share what the spirits show you.
name: Far Seer
restoreCry: The sight returns. The elements speak through you.

breakCry: Da voodoo be weak, mon! Brew, bones and spirit.
fantasy: A Darkspear mystic of wards and brews, rattling bone charms and flinging foul concoctions. Da voodoo provides.
honorRules: Brew your own potions and drink them proudly — Alchemy is your craft.
Drop totems like wards in every fight.
Collect trophies from worthy kills (keep a bone or skull in your bags).
name: Witch Doctor
restoreCry: Da voodoo be strong again, mon!

breakCry: Mutiny! A buccaneer fights with cutlass and pistol or not at all.
fantasy: A swaggering corsair with a cutlass on the hip and a pistol in the belt. Gold, grog and glory — in that order.
honorRules: Cutlass in hand, pistol in the belt — sword or dagger, plus a gun.
Loot every kill; never leave gold on a body.
Eat, drink and be merry before every voyage (buff food and drink).
name: Buccaneer
restoreCry: Back aboard, captain. The horizon is yours.
source: Warcraft RPG / Pirates of the South Seas

breakCry: The formula is compromised! A Potion Doc trusts the brew and the blade — nothing else.
fantasy: A Gnomeregan chemist with poisoned daggers and a bandolier of brews — equal parts surgeon, poisoner and walking laboratory. Somewhere in all that glassware is the cure for a city.
honorRules: Keep both blades poisoned from the day the trainer teaches you the craft — the chemistry does the killing.
Brew your own: potions, elixirs and antidotes from your own bench, and a swig of the newest batch before every dangerous fight.
Pick every herb you pass and pocket every venom sac — everything green is research, everything venomous a reagent.
name: Potion Doc
restoreCry: The mixture stabilizes. Science resumes.

suggested.name: Blackvenom Blade
suggested.note: Rohh the Silent, a rare blade above Render's Valley — the venom already in the steel

suggested.name: Assassin's Blade
suggested.note: World drop — the trade's plain instrument, honest steel for the second hand

suggested.name: Herbalist's Gloves
suggested.note: Leatherworked, +5 Herbalism — commission a friend; the doc supplies the formula

suggested.name: Heart of Noxxion
suggested.note: Noxxion, Maraudon — an antidote in a jar, cut from a creature of pure poison

suggested.name: Distracting Dagger
suggested.note: Prince Tortheldrin, Dire Maul — a lecturer's pointer for the off hand

suggested.name: Cadaverous Armor
suggested.note: Scholomance — the mortician's apron, sized down and worn with intent

suggested.name: Felstriker
suggested.note: Warchief Rend Blackhand, Blackrock Spire — three seconds in which no dose misses

trials.epitaph: The decontamination log recorded %d of the fallen city's sick, given rest.
trials.name: The Long Decontamination
trials.objective: Put the fallen of Gnomeregan to rest — leper gnomes, the Leprous and the Irradiated, wherever the sickness shambles
trials.text: Thermaplugg's bomb did not kill eight gnomes in ten. It left most of them walking. There is no antidote yet for what your people became — so until you brew one, mercy is the only medicine, and every dose is delivered on a blade. Log each one. The cure will want data.

trials.name: Know Your Enemy
trials.objective: Slay 10 Rockjaw troggs in Dun Morogh
trials.text: It was troggs that breached the lower vaults and troggs the bomb was meant for. The Rockjaw tribe squats in the hills above Coldridge as if the experiment ended well. Ten specimens, terminated and noted. Science begins with observation.

trials.name: The First Brew
trials.objective: Brew a Minor Healing Potion in Tinker Town, Ironforge
trials.text: Every great work starts with a clean bench. Beg space at Berryfizz's in Tinker Town, among your own exiled people, and bottle your first dose of mended flesh. Smell it. Remember it. That is what you are for.

trials.name: The Oath of the Exile
trials.objective: /kneel before High Tinker Mekkatorque in Tinker Town
trials.text: Gelbin Mekkatorque signed the order that gassed his own city, because his most trusted friend falsified the data. He carries it every day, in a borrowed corner of a dwarven mountain. Kneel to him there — and swear the exile's oath: the city will be clean again, and the data will be honest.

trials.name: The First Garden
trials.objective: Gather 20 Peacebloom with your own hands
trials.text: A chemist who buys reagents is a customer, not a scientist. Twenty Peacebloom, picked by your own fingers from the snow-edges and roadsides. Everything green is research. Everything white and small doubly so.

trials.name: The Sealed Door
trials.objective: /mourn before the gates of Gnomeregan
trials.text: Go west past Brewnall, to where the doors of the greatest city ever engineered stand sealed from the inside. Behind them: your laboratories, your libraries, and most of your family, still walking. Mourn properly. Then take out your notebook, because grief is also data.

trials.name: Raw Material
trials.objective: Harvest 5 Small Venom Sacs from the spiders of the hills
trials.text: Nature has been running toxicology trials for ten thousand years; a Potion Doc merely reads her notes. Open the spiders of Dun Morogh and Loch Modan carefully and take five venom sacs intact. Waste no specimen.

trials.completionNote: His journal goes to SI:7. His poisons go in your bag. Peer review.
trials.name: The Rival's Tower
trials.objective: Slay Klaven Mortwake in his tower in Westfall — alone, vows clean
trials.text: SI:7 sends every young poisoner to one door: Klaven Mortwake, the Defias chemist buying Venture Company formulas in his tower in the Dust Plains — dead things walking his yard that no honest medicine made. Go alone, vows clean, and close his laboratory permanently. The first rule of the craft: there are wrong hands, and chemistry must not be in them.

trials.name: The First Fang
trials.objective: Take and wield a Venom Web Fang from the spiders of Duskwood
trials.text: In Duskwood the Venom Web spiders grow fangs the length of your forearm, and the venom channel runs true enough to use unmodified. Pull one, clean it, hilt it. Your first proper instrument — half dagger, half syringe.

trials.name: Trial of the Crippled Step
trials.objective: Slow 50 foes with Crippling Poison
trials.text: Any thug can make a blade hurt. A toxicologist makes it govern: a dose on the edge, and the subject's own blood carries the instruction — slower, slower, stop. Fifty field trials, fifty limping witnesses. Publish on steel.

trials.name: The Blinding Crop
trials.objective: Gather 10 Fadeleaf
trials.text: Fadeleaf hides in the shade it makes — fitting, since ground fine it becomes the powder that buys a rogue three free seconds of darkness. Ten leaves, picked whole. The garden arms you better than any armory.

trials.name: The Escape Protocol
trials.objective: Brew a Free Action Potion at your own bench
trials.text: A dead doc files no reports. This is the brew that ignores the net, the snare, the stunning blow — thirty seconds in which the world may not hold you. Brew it yourself and keep one on the belt always. On Hardcore, the escape protocol is not optional equipment.

trials.name: The Clean Zone
trials.objective: Stand in the Clean Zone of Gnomeregan
trials.text: Inside the fallen city one chamber still scrubs its own air — the Clean Zone, where the survivors huddle and the radiation meters finally quiet. Stand in it. Breathe. This is what the whole city smelled like once, and will again.

trials.name: The Breach
trials.objective: Slay Grubbis in Gnomeregan
trials.text: Follow the old service tunnels to where the first wall fell. The trogg Grubbis holds the breach with a pet basilisk and the confidence of something too dense to poison. The infection began at this wound; begin the cure there too.

trials.name: What the Bomb Left
trials.objective: Claim the Toxic Revenger from Viscous Fallout in Gnomeregan
trials.text: In the flooded sumps below the city, the radiation itself has pulled together a body and started walking. Boil Viscous Fallout down and take the blade it leaves: a dagger that sweats the city's own poison. Name it evidence. Carry the bomb's signature into every fight until the debt is paid.

trials.completionNote: “Usurpers! Gnomeregan is mine!” he screamed. The data, as ever, disagreed with him.
trials.name: The Mad King
trials.objective: Slay Mekgineer Thermaplugg on his throne, vows clean
trials.text: At the bottom of everything sits Sicco Thermaplugg, who falsified one report and murdered a nation, and now plays king over the leprous and the dead. He expected thirty percent. Walk into his throne room with your vows clean and show him a margin of error.

trials.name: Transmutation
trials.objective: Craft a Philosopher's Stone
trials.text: In Gadgetzan, the goblin Pestlezugg sells the old transmuter's secret to anyone with the skill to survive it. Iron into gold, one element into another — the stone in your pack says chemistry has no fixed walls, only unfunded experiments. Every master of the craft has made one. Make yours.

trials.name: The Pale Harvest
trials.objective: Gather 10 Ghost Mushrooms from the dark places of the world
trials.text: Ghost Mushrooms grow only where the sun has given up — cave mouths in the Hinterlands, the poisoned deeps of Maraudon. They glow faintly, like an apology. Ten caps, gathered by lamplight. The best reagents are the ones that almost kill you to collect.

trials.name: A Study in Pure Poison
trials.objective: Slay Noxxion in Maraudon
trials.text: Deep in Maraudon something has happened that should interest you professionally: corruption so concentrated it no longer needs a body to poison. Noxxion is venom with ambition. Take it apart and see how it works — and bottle the heart; the antidote literature is thin.

trials.name: Bad Alchemy
trials.objective: Dissolve 25 Cursed and Tainted Oozes in Felwood
trials.text: Felwood is what a laboratory looks like when nobody cleans up: the Legion's runoff pooled, curdled, and learned to crawl. Twenty-five oozes rendered down to harmless slime. Consider it professional embarrassment — someone's experiment escaped, and chemists bury their own mistakes.

trials.name: The Colleague
trials.objective: /thank Alchemist Arbington at Chillwind Camp
trials.text: At Chillwind Camp a human named Arbington holds the line against the Scourge's plague with a mortar, a pestle and no sleep. He will hand you wards and counteragents and ask nothing back. Thank him properly. The fraternity of the bench is small, and older than any Alliance.

trials.name: The Cauldron War
trials.objective: Destroy 4 Cauldron Lords at the plague cauldrons of the Western Plaguelands
trials.text: Across the Western Plaguelands the Scourge brews its plague openly, in farm-field cauldrons each stirred by a bound geist of the vapors. This is chemistry as atrocity — your discipline, weaponized against the living. Take the field stations one by one. Leave the cauldrons fit for honest work.

trials.name: Rite of the Perfect Dose
trials.objective: Slay an elite enemy while your own Elixir of the Mongoose runs in your veins
trials.text: The old goblin alchemists rode to war on a chemically induced rage. Yours is more refined: the Elixir of the Mongoose, brewed at your own bench, every reflex sharpened to a laboratory edge. Drink your finest work and fell an elite foe while it holds. You are the experiment, and the result.

trials.completionNote: The Sarkhoffs, his victims, can rest now. The title 'Doctor' is yours to redeem.
trials.name: The Other Doctor
trials.objective: Slay Doctor Theolen Krastinov in Scholomance, vows clean
trials.text: In the Scholomance works a man who kept your title and burned everything it means: Doctor Theolen Krastinov, the Butcher, who renders his patients into teaching material. He is what you are one broken vow away from becoming. Go in with yours clean, and retire him from practice.

trials.name: The Black Lotus
trials.objective: Find and pick a Black Lotus
trials.text: Somewhere in the burnt and frozen corners of the world, the rarest herb in existence is blooming right now, and a hundred herbalists are racing you to it. The Black Lotus crowns every flask that matters. Pick one with your own hand. There is no other way to hold one honestly.

trials.name: The Master's Draught
trials.objective: Brew a Flask of the Titans
trials.text: Three hundred skill, a flask cut from crystal, the Black Lotus dissolved at last — and the result is a draught that makes a body briefly titanic, and does not even fade in death. This is the summit of the bench. Brew it once and you may call yourself master in any guildhall in the world.

trials.name: A Venom Out of Legend
trials.objective: Wield a fang out of legend — the Gutgore Ripper, a Core Hound Tooth, or Perdition's Blade
trials.text: Past the end of the war, the Molten Core keeps the reference collection: the Gutgore Ripper, whose wound unmakes the whole patient; the Core Hound Tooth, pulled from the jaw of the Firelord's own kennel; and Perdition's Blade from Ragnaros's hand, venom replaced with fire. Any one of them completes a poisoner's education. Be few.

trials.name: The Last Epidemic
trials.objective: Slay Hakkar the Soulflayer in Zul'Gurub
trials.text: In Zul'Gurub waits the Blood God, whose corrupted blood is the most virulent agent ever recorded — one drop walked out of that temple once and emptied whole cities. There is no grander rounds a doctor will ever make. Drain the source. End the last epidemic before it begins.

trials.name: One for Gnomeregan
trials.objective: /drink at Brewnall Village, within sight of the gates
trials.text: Go back to Brewnall, where the exiles drink with their backs to the lake and the sealed doors just up the hill. Raise something you brewed yourself — for the city, the dead, the walking, and the day the meters read zero. The cure is coming. You are the proof.

breakCry: The flame gutters! Fire and powder, nothing else — burn, or be snuffed out.

chapters.title: Chapter IV — The Heart of the Flame

fantasy: A goggled mage who solved magic the way an engineer solves everything: pick the school that explodes and master nothing else. Every problem is a fire problem — some just need a bomb first.

forbidAuras.label: No rime on a pyromancer's skin

honorRules: Let no killing spell leave your hands but fire — Frostbolt and Arcane Missiles stay untrained forever. The other schools may carry you; they may never kill for you.
Engineering is your second school: open every dangerous fight with a bomb from your own bench.
